title: Athysanus pusillus
intro: Athysanus pusillus, also known as the dwarf athysanus or dwarf sandwort, is a small, annual herbaceous plant belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Athysanus pusillus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Athysanus pusillus is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Athysanus pusillus is classified within the family Brassicaceae. It is a member of the genus Athysanus, which is part of the order Brassicales. This plant belongs to the class Magnoliopsida and the division Magnoliophyta. At the kingdom level, Athysanus pusillus is classified as Plantae. It is also known as the dwarf sandwort, and its classification reflects its botanical relationships.
